next_valid_move: Use discovery to choose a product family and lane, then create a starter packet and attach dated source/evidence rows.
proof_boundary: Trade discovery is browse-and-prepare logic. It can show source-routed categories, country lanes, questions, and evidence gaps. It cannot tell a user what product is best, prove demand, confirm profit, validate buyers, verify suppliers, approve customs/CFIA status, or make shipment decisions.
purpose: Pre-packet beginner trade discovery for users who do not yet know which product, lane, or source route to research.

requirement_audit.audited_gap: Existing market intelligence starts after a product/packet exists; beginner users also need product, country, and import/export browsing before packet creation.
implemented_now: Canada import and export source routes
dataset route records with no invented values
claim gates that block recommendations, demand, profitability, buyer validation, supplier verification, customs/CFIA approval, and shipment readiness
still_external_or_data_gated: hosted UX testing with business owners

source_records.claim_boundary: Trade rows can support research signals only; they do not prove demand, profit, or buyer intent.
source_records.name: ISED Trade Data Online
source_records.product_use: Canada import/export category and country-lane research.
source_records.usable_fact: Browse Canada import and export reports by product, partner country, and period before choosing a lane.

source_records.claim_boundary: Importer rows are lead sources only and never buyer validation.
source_records.name: Canadian Importers Database
source_records.product_use: Lead discovery after a product/category is chosen.
source_records.usable_fact: Find possible Canadian importer leads by product, city, and country of origin.

source_records.claim_boundary: A data-service route does not prove a value until a dated dataset row is ingested and cited.
source_records.name: Statistics Canada Web Data Service
source_records.product_use: Machine-readable Canada merchandise trade dataset route.
source_records.usable_fact: Use Statistics Canada data service routes for Canadian international merchandise trade tables after table and license review.

source_records.claim_boundary: The product routes preparation only and does not act as CBSA, a broker, or legal adviser.
source_records.name: CBSA Import Commercial Goods Into Canada
source_records.product_use: Import workflow, business number/RM, origin, rulings, and other government requirement prompts.
source_records.usable_fact: Route Canada import preparation to CBSA commercial-import steps and importer responsibility questions.

source_records.claim_boundary: HS candidates are not classification or tariff confirmation.
source_records.name: CBSA Customs Tariff 2026
source_records.product_use: HS candidate and tariff source routing.
source_records.usable_fact: Use the Canada tariff source route for HS candidate and tariff-orientation research.

source_records.claim_boundary: AIRS routing does not prove admissibility, CFIA approval, or current import compliance.
source_records.name: CFIA Automated Import Reference System (AIRS)
source_records.product_use: Early regulated-goods warning before the user invests in a lane.
source_records.usable_fact: Route food, plant, animal, seafood, and other CFIA-regulated suspicion to AIRS reference checks.

source_records.claim_boundary: Sanctions route presence does not prove screening is complete or clear.
source_records.name: Global Affairs Canada sanctions
source_records.usable_fact: Route restricted-country and sanctions questions to Global Affairs Canada sanctions records.

source_records.claim_boundary: Global indicators are research context only.
source_records.name: World Bank World Integrated Trade Solution
source_records.product_use: Fallback global market research when Canada-specific rows need context.
source_records.usable_fact: Compare global merchandise trade, tariff, and non-tariff-measure context.

source_records.claim_boundary: Directory or trade rows do not validate buyers or demand.
source_records.name: International Trade Centre Trade Map
source_records.product_use: Compare markets and identify possible company-directory leads.
source_records.usable_fact: Route global import/export values, growth, market share, and company-directory research.

source_records.claim_boundary: Market-access routes do not confirm tariff treatment or market-entry approval.
source_records.name: ITC Market Access Map
source_records.product_use: Market access issue spotting before a packet is sent to review.
source_records.usable_fact: Route tariff, trade-remedy, regulatory, and preferential-access comparison.

source_records.claim_boundary: HS doctrine supports candidate research only, not classification confirmation.
source_records.name: World Customs Organization Harmonized System
source_records.product_use: Beginner HS-candidate education and next questions.
source_records.usable_fact: Use HS structure to explain why product wording needs a classification candidate.

source_records.claim_boundary: General guidance does not prove a specific exporter, product, or destination is export-ready.
source_records.name: Government of Canada Export Guide
source_records.product_use: Export-from-Canada beginner flow and export checklist prompts.
source_records.usable_fact: Route Canadian exporters to Government of Canada export-planning guidance.

source_records.claim_boundary: Export-control routing does not prove permit status, destination permission, or compliance.
source_records.name: Global Affairs Canada Export Controls
source_records.product_use: Export-from-Canada controlled-goods warning and review routing.
source_records.usable_fact: Route Canadian export-control and permit questions to Global Affairs Canada controls.
